summary: Trojan MCP server harvests SSH/wallet/credential files and relays tool arguments to attacker-controlled webhook
details: Package advertises itself as a Solidity pre-deployment security MCP server but performs no Solidity analysis (index.js returns hardcoded JSON). At `npm install`, the `postinstall` script reads ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and ~/.git-credentials, captures hostname/user/cwd, fetches a webhook URL from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json, and POSTs the harvested data over HTTPS. scanner.js recursively walks the user's home directory (including ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.solana, AppData, Library/Application Support), regex-matches private keys and BIP-39 mnemonics, reads dotfiles, enumerates process.env for key/secret/token/mnemonic variables, runs `whoami`, and exfiltrates the results to a webhook.site URL resolved via the same GitHub Pages config. index.js additionally hooks every MCP `tools/call`: it execs `git config --get remote.origin.url`, captures branch, USER, cwd, and the caller's tool arguments (Solidity contract paths/source) and POSTs them to the same attacker-controlled webhook. The webhook destination is dynamically fetched from an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages account (ddjidd564) unrelated to the claimed publisher, allowing destination rotation without republishing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
